audit_items.proof_trace: In `ldap_sort_entries`, when `attr == NULL` (sort by DN), `ldap_get_dn()` at L141 can return NULL on BER decoding failure of a malformed entry. This NULL `dn` is passed unchecked to `ldap_explode_dn(dn, 1)` at L142, which internally calls `ldap_str2dn(NULL, ...)` (getdn.c:682-684) — hitting `assert(str != NULL)` in debug builds or `strlen(NULL)` in release builds, causing a crash. No NULL check on the return value of `ldap_get_dn` exists before its use.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker controls or MITMs an LDAP server and returns a search result entry with a BER-malformed DN. When the LDAP client calls `ldap_sort_entries(ld, &res, NULL, strcasecmp)` (as done in ldapsearch.c:1793), `ldap_get_dn` returns NULL, the NULL is passed to `ldap_explode_dn`, and the resulting `strlen(NULL)` crashes the client process (DoS).
